label: ClickHouse
description: Connect to a ClickHouse columnar OLAP database for high-performance analytical queries.

form.title: Connect ClickHouse
form.subtitle: Enter your ClickHouse host, credentials, and database to start querying.

methods.label: ClickHouse Cloud
methods.description: Connect to a managed ClickHouse Cloud service instance. Uses HTTPS (port 8443) and a native ClickHouse user with password. Recommended for ClickHouse Cloud customers.
methods.how_to:
## Connect to ClickHouse Cloud

1. Sign in to [ClickHouse Cloud Console](https://console.clickhouse.cloud/).
2. Open your service, then go to **Connect → View connection details**.
3. Select **Native** or **HTTPS** from the protocol tabs — for Anton, use **HTTPS**.
4. Copy the **Host** (e.g. `abc123.us-east-1.aws.clickhouse.cloud`) and **Port** (default `8443`).
5. Under **Security → Database credentials**, create or copy an existing user. Note the **Username** and **Password** (shown only on creation — save it somewhere safe).
6. Enter your **Database** name (default is `default` if you haven't created one).
7. Paste all values into the fields below and click **Submit**.

### Notes

- ClickHouse Cloud enforces TLS — leave **Use HTTPS** checked.
- If you reset the password in the console, update the password field in Anton to avoid auth errors.
- Firewall rules live under **Settings → Network access**; make sure your IP is allowed.

fields.name: host
fields.label: Host
fields.description: Your ClickHouse Cloud service hostname, found in Connect → View connection details.

fields.name: port
fields.label: Port
fields.description: HTTPS port for ClickHouse Cloud is 8443 by default.

fields.name: database
fields.label: Database
fields.description: The ClickHouse database name to connect to.

fields.name: user
fields.label: Username
fields.description: ClickHouse user with access to the target database.

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: Password for the ClickHouse user. Stored securely and never sent to Anton servers.

fields.name: use_https
fields.label: Use HTTPS (TLS)
fields.description: Enable TLS/HTTPS for the connection. Required for ClickHouse Cloud; leave on for most setups.

methods.label: Self-Hosted ClickHouse
methods.description: Connect to a ClickHouse server you manage on your own infrastructure. Supports both HTTP (8123) and HTTPS (8443) and custom ports.
methods.how_to:
## Connect to a Self-Hosted ClickHouse Instance

1. Confirm your ClickHouse server is running and reachable from this machine.
2. Find your **host** — either a hostname (`clickhouse.internal`) or IP address.
3. Determine the port:
   - HTTP (no TLS): **8123** (default)
   - HTTPS (with TLS): **8443** (default)
   - Custom ports are set in `config.xml` under `<http_port>` or `<https_port>`.
4. Note the **database** you want to query (use `default` if unsure).
5. Identify a **username** and **password** with access. ClickHouse ships with a `default` user — check `users.xml` or `users.d/` for credentials.
6. If using HTTPS, ensure your server has a valid TLS certificate or that self-signed certs are handled (Anton will warn on untrusted certs).
7. Fill in all fields below and click **Submit**.

### Notes

- Toggle **Use HTTPS** off if your server only listens on plain HTTP.
- For Docker-based setups, make sure the port is exposed (`-p 8123:8123` or `-p 8443:8443`).
- Firewall or security-group rules must allow inbound traffic on the chosen port from your machine's IP.

fields.name: host
fields.label: Host
fields.description: Hostname or IP address of your ClickHouse server.

fields.name: port
fields.label: Port
fields.description: HTTP port is 8123; HTTPS port is 8443. Change if your server uses a custom port.

fields.name: database
fields.label: Database
fields.description: The ClickHouse database name to connect to.

fields.name: user
fields.label: Username
fields.description: ClickHouse user with read (and optionally write) access to the target database.

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: Password for the ClickHouse user. Leave blank if your server allows passwordless access (not recommended).

fields.name: use_https
fields.label: Use HTTPS (TLS)
fields.description: Enable to connect over HTTPS/TLS. Requires your server to be configured with a TLS certificate.
